verses.text: The east gate is shut, for 'the LORD... hath entered in by it'; only the prince may sit there to eat bread. A sacred entrance. Where God's glory has passed, the place is set apart and honoured.
The east gate is shut, for 'the LORD... hath entered in by it'; only the prince may sit there to eat bread. A sacred entrance. Where God's glory has passed, the place is set apart and honoured.
The east gate is shut, for 'the LORD... hath entered in by it'; only the prince may sit there to eat bread. A sacred entrance. Where God's glory has passed, the place is set apart and honoured.

The glory again fills the house, and God bids him 'mark well' the ordinances, rebuking the bringing of strangers into the sanctuary. Reverence demanded. God's worship is not to be mixed with the profane.

The glory again fills the house, and God bids him 'mark well' the ordinances, rebuking the bringing of strangers into the sanctuary. Reverence demanded. God's worship is not to be mixed with the profane.

The glory again fills the house, and God bids him 'mark well' the ordinances, rebuking the bringing of strangers into the sanctuary. Reverence demanded. God's worship is not to be mixed with the profane.
They had set 'uncircumcised in heart' within His sanctuary; no such stranger shall enter it. Heart-holiness. Outward access without inward consecration profanes God's house.
They had set 'uncircumcised in heart' within His sanctuary; no such stranger shall enter it. Heart-holiness. Outward access without inward consecration profanes God's house.
They had set 'uncircumcised in heart' within His sanctuary; no such stranger shall enter it. Heart-holiness. Outward access without inward consecration profanes God's house.
The Levites who strayed after idols shall bear their iniquity and serve only in lesser tasks. Demoted, not cast off. Past unfaithfulness has its lasting consequence in service.
The Levites who strayed after idols shall bear their iniquity and serve only in lesser tasks. Demoted, not cast off. Past unfaithfulness has its lasting consequence in service.
The Levites who strayed after idols shall bear their iniquity and serve only in lesser tasks. Demoted, not cast off. Past unfaithfulness has its lasting consequence in service.
They shall not draw near as priests but keep the charge of the house for its ordinary service. Limited ministry. God measures nearness to Himself by faithfulness.
They shall not draw near as priests but keep the charge of the house for its ordinary service. Limited ministry. God measures nearness to Himself by faithfulness.
But the sons of Zadok, who kept charge when Israel went astray, shall come near to minister to Him. Faithfulness rewarded. Those who held firm in apostasy are given the closest service.
But the sons of Zadok, who kept charge when Israel went astray, shall come near to minister to Him. Faithfulness rewarded. Those who held firm in apostasy are given the closest service.
They shall wear linen, not wool, in the inner court, and not sanctify the people by their garments. Pure ministry. The servants of God are marked by purity, even in dress.
They shall wear linen, not wool, in the inner court, and not sanctify the people by their garments. Pure ministry. The servants of God are marked by purity, even in dress.
They shall wear linen, not wool, in the inner court, and not sanctify the people by their garments. Pure ministry. The servants of God are marked by purity, even in dress.
They shall not shave their heads nor drink wine in service, and shall marry only virgins or priests' widows. Sobriety and order. God's ministers are to be holy in person and home.
They shall not shave their heads nor drink wine in service, and shall marry only virgins or priests' widows. Sobriety and order. God's ministers are to be holy in person and home.
They shall not shave their heads nor drink wine in service, and shall marry only virgins or priests' widows. Sobriety and order. God's ministers are to be holy in person and home.
They shall teach His people the difference between holy and profane and judge by His judgments. Teaching priests. To distinguish the holy is a chief duty of God's servants.
They shall teach His people the difference between holy and profane and judge by His judgments. Teaching priests. To distinguish the holy is a chief duty of God's servants.
They shall not defile themselves with the dead, save near kin, and after cleansing offer a sin offering. Guarded purity. Even necessary contact with death requires renewed cleansing.
They shall not defile themselves with the dead, save near kin, and after cleansing offer a sin offering. Guarded purity. Even necessary contact with death requires renewed cleansing.
They shall not defile themselves with the dead, save near kin, and after cleansing offer a sin offering. Guarded purity. Even necessary contact with death requires renewed cleansing.
'I am their inheritance'; they shall eat the offerings and avoid what dies of itself. God their portion. The servant who has the Lord needs no other inheritance.
'I am their inheritance'; they shall eat the offerings and avoid what dies of itself. God their portion. The servant who has the Lord needs no other inheritance.
